title: Intuit Inc. (INTU) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Intuit Inc. (INTU). Economic Prospect Score: 72/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Intuit Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 12% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.5% WACC balances Intuit Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisParagraphs: Intuit Inc. (INTU) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 12% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 9.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Intuit Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 12% FCF growth and 9.5% WACC, Opus calculates INTU's intrinsic value at approximately $612.96 per share, suggesting the stock is fairly valued at $650.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive INTU's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (12%) and discount rate (9.5%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: TurboTax and QuickBooks create a tax-and-bookkeeping ecosystem for SMBs and consumers. Credit Karma adds financial services cross-sell. AI assistants enhance product value. Growth is steady at 12-15%.
prospectKeyRisks: IRS Direct File expansion potentially disrupting TurboTax's consumer tax preparation monopoly
SMB churn increasing in economic downturn as small businesses fail or cut software spending
AI tax filing assistants from competitors reducing Intuit's differentiation
prospectKeyCatalysts: AI-powered financial assistant becoming the default small business CFO tool, increasing ARPU
Credit Karma monetization through financial product recommendations growing 20%+
International expansion of QuickBooks into underserved SMB markets
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
